rulings.Case Title: DOMENIC J CANNIZZARO et al VS. FRED P RICHARD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 6, 2011, line 6. Defendants Carmel Richard and Robert Richard¿s motion for summary adjudication is denied as to all claims sought to be summarily adjudicated except the fourth cause of action in the Cannizzaros¿ first amended complaint in case no. 494624. There are triable issues whether CCP 340.6 applies to the claims sought to be summarily adjudicated. Moreover, the moving parties¿ separate statement is not in compliance with CRC 3.1350(d) by failing to identify what claims each issue relates to, thereby making it very hard to understand the relationship between the purported undisputed facts and each claim sought to be summarily adjudicated. No opposition was filed by the Cannizzaros. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion In Support Of Pltfs Motion To Quash The Subpoenas
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 6, 2011, Line 5. PLAINTIFF KIM RYDEN'S Notice Of Motion and Motion To Quash The Subpoenas. Plaintiff¿s motion to quash subpoenas is granted as to Qualys and denied as to Dr. Zeitz. The records sought from Dr. Zeitz are directly relevant to plaintiff¿s claims, while those sought from Qualys are not. The records obtained from Dr. Zeitz must be used only for purposes of this case and distributed only to persons working on the case with a need to know the information contained in those records. rulings.Case Title: SEAN DEVRIES VS. SHREE KRISHNA HOSPITALITY, INC. et al*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO SACRAMENTO COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Change Venue And For Attorneys Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 6, 2011, line 19. Defendants Dhruv Shah and Nahesh Shah¿s motion to change venue is granted. The proper county for a claim seeking involuntary dissolution of a corporation is the county where the corporation¿s principal office is located. Defendants are entitled to have the action heard in that county even if other claims are alleged. Defendants¿ request for fees is denied. There is no indication that plaintiff acted in bad faith. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/HEK)
